title: Slope from graph
description: Find the slope of a line on the coordinate plane.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the slope of the line?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To measure slope, we pick any two points on the line. Then we look at the horizontal and vertical distances between those points.



[[☃ image 1]]
The slope of the line is $1$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x- and y-axes each scale by one. A graph of a line intersects the points negative one, zero and zero, one.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]



**What is the slope of the line?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To measure slope, we pick any two points on the line. Then we look at the horizontal and vertical distances between those points.



[[☃ image 1]]

Going from the point on the left to the point on the right, the change in $y$ is $\purpleD 2$, and the change in $x$ is $\redD 3$.

$\text{Slope}=\dfrac{\purpleD{\text{rise}}}{\redD{\text{run}}}=\dfrac{\purpleD{\Delta y}}{\redD{\Delta x}}=\dfrac{\purpleD{2}}{\redD{3}}$
The slope of the line is $\dfrac{2}{3}$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x- and y-axes each scale by one. A graph of a line intersects the points one, two and four, four.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the slope of the line?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To measure slope, we pick any two points on the line. Then we look at the horizontal and vertical distances between those points.



[[☃ image 1]]

Going from the point on the left to the point on the right, the change in $y$ is $\purpleD 1$, and the change in $x$ is $\redD 2$.

$\text{Slope}=\dfrac{\purpleD{\text{rise}}}{\redD{\text{run}}}=\dfrac{\purpleD{\Delta y}}{\redD{\Delta x}}=\dfrac{\purpleD{1}}{\redD{2}}$
The slope of the line is $\dfrac{1}{2}$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x- and y-axes each scale by one. A graph of a line intersects the points negative three, two and negative one, three.
